name: Can I get workers' comp for back pain from lifting boxes if I have some arthritis already?

mainEntity.name: Can I get workers' comp for back pain from lifting boxes if I have some arthritis already?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Workers' comp covers the aggravation of pre-existing conditions like arthritis. If your repetitive lifting made your underlying arthritis symptomatic or worse, the employer is still responsible for your medical care and benefits.
acceptedAnswer.description:
Yes, having arthritis does not disqualify you from workers' compensation. Under Utah and Idaho law, if your daily work tasks "aggravated," "accelerated," or "exacerbated" a pre-existing condition, it is a compensable injury. You don't have to be a perfect physical specimen to get coverage; the employer takes you "as they find you."

The challenge is proving that the work lifting—not just the natural progression of arthritis—is what made the medical treatment necessary.

Key Factors:
- Was the arthritis asymptomatic before the job?
- Did a specific work event or repetitive task make it worse?
- Medical testimony comparing "pre-injury" vs "post-injury" X-rays or MRIs.

You will need a doctor who can clearly state that your work duties caused a "lighting up" of your arthritis. 

An attorney is crucial here to ensure the insurance company doesn't use the word "arthritis" as a "get out of jail free" card to deny your entire claim.
